title: Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation
summary: A low-movement procedure for a trapped person to protect breathing, control dust, signal rescuers, and communicate location without causing further collapse.
content:
A low-movement procedure for a trapped person to protect breathing, control dust, signal rescuers, and communicate location without causing further collapse.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: whistle or hard object for tapping
steps: Stop unnecessary movement, cover nose and mouth loosely, and check for severe bleeding you can control with direct pressure.
Use a phone message or emergency call when a signal exists; state the address, floor, nearby room, injuries, sounds, and hazards.
Signal in short repeated groups with a whistle or tapping on a pipe or wall, then stay quiet to hear rescuers.
Protect battery and air: dim the screen, avoid shouting except when rescuers are close, and do not light flames or disturb load-bearing debris.
warnings: Do not crawl blindly through unstable voids.
Dust, gas, fire, water, or worsening breathing require immediate reporting to rescuers.
common_mistakes: Starting Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Trapped under debris: signaling and air conservation.']
